action: open Google Chrome and open a new tab and type 'youtube' in the search bar
fallback_action: Perform a Google search using Internet Explorer

recipe.steps: is the first action. The task is to open Google Chrome, open a new tab, and type 'youtube' in the search bar. On the current screen, I can see the Google Chrome web browser icon (Box ID 92). The first step is to open Google Chrome by clicking on its icon.

previous action: I was instructed to open Google Chrome by clicking its icon. Looking at the current screen, I can see the Google Chrome browser window is now open (Box ID 151, 'Microsoft Edge browser.' is also present, but Chrome is the one that was just opened). The next step is to open a new tab in Chrome. The standard way to do this is by using the hotkey Ctrl+T.

previous action: I used the hotkey Ctrl+T to open a new tab in Google Chrome. The current screen shows a new tab page in Chrome, with the search bar visible (Box ID 7, 'Search Google or type a URL'). This confirms the previous action succeeded. The next step is to type 'youtube' in the search bar.

previous action: I was instructed to type 'youtube' in the search bar of a new Chrome tab. Looking at the current screen, I can see that 'youtube' is now present in the search bar (Box ID 0 and Box ID 6 both show 'youtube'). This confirms the previous action succeeded. The next logical step to complete the search is to either press 'Enter' or click the 'Google Search' button (Box ID 1). Pressing 'Enter' is the standard and efficient way to submit the search.
